items.question: What is generalization in supervised ML?
options: Performing well on unseen data from the same problem
Minimizing loss only on the training set
Using more GPU memory during fit()
items.answer: Performing well on unseen data from the same problem
items.explanation: Generalization is unseen-data performance — not training-set memorization.

items.question: Which metric best estimates generalization before deployment?
options: Test accuracy after fit(X_train, y_train) and predict(X_test)
items.answer: Test accuracy after fit(X_train, y_train) and predict(X_test)
items.explanation: Held-out test data the model never trained on proxies unseen production performance.

items.question: Train error = 0.02, test error = 0.38. What does this suggest?
options: Likely overfitting — poor generalization with a large gap
items.answer: Likely overfitting — poor generalization with a large gap
items.explanation: Low train error with much higher test error is a classic generalization gap from overfitting.

items.question: What is the generalization gap?
options: Test (or validation) error minus train error
items.answer: Test (or validation) error minus train error
items.explanation: Gap = test − train; a large positive gap with low train error signals weak generalization.

items.question: Why fit a scaler on X_train only before transforming X_test?
options: To prevent leakage so test rows remain truly unseen for generalization estimates
items.answer: To prevent leakage so test rows remain truly unseen for generalization estimates
items.explanation: Statistics learned from test data leak information — held-out metrics would be optimistically biased.

items.question: A model achieves 100% training accuracy on randomly shuffled labels. Test accuracy stays near 50%. What happened?
options: The model memorized noise — no real pattern generalization
The test set was too small
items.answer: The model memorized noise — no real pattern generalization
items.explanation: Random labels have no signal; memorization yields high train score but chance-level test performance.
